page: Amazon Web Services i3.large is a storage-optimized instance featuring the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor operating at 2.3 GHz, delivering 2 dedicated vCPUs from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. The instance includes 15.0 GB of system memory, translating to 7.62 GB per core. Notably, it comes bundled with 475 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, while network performance is rated at a baseline of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the compute and memory subsystems operate in lower performance tiers. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, along with Geekbench and Passmark CPU scores, fall into the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth tests, including large block read and write operations, also register in the bottom 10% category. However, the primary value proposition of this instance lies in its high-speed local NVMe storage capacity rather than raw computational throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i3.large offers a dense local storage allocation relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for storage-intensive workloads that do not require high CPU performance. It is suited for local database hosting, caching layers, and basic storage-heavy applications where high IOPS and low-latency local storage are prioritized over processing speed.
description: Amazon Web Services i3.large is a storage-optimized instance utilizing the x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs, 15.0 GB of memory, and a bundled 475 GB NVMe SSD, operating on the Xen hypervisor. Benchmark data indicates that its CPU and memory performance, including stress-ng and Geekbench scores, fall within the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Despite these computational limitations, the instance provides a high-speed local storage footprint with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. This hardware profile makes the i3.large qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-heavy tasks that prioritize local NVMe capacity over raw processing power. It is best suited for lightweight database hosting, caching layers, and basic storage-intensive workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i3.large is a storage-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 15.0 GB of RAM, and 475 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for lightweight database and storage-heavy workloads.

tagline: A storage-optimized instance featuring dedicated compute resources and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for localized data workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs
Bundled 475 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Best suited for lightweight database hosting and local caching
